name: modify_bid
description: Change the amount of a bid already prepared/placed on a booking. Same as place_bid but for updating an existing bid — pass the sessionId, the NEW amount, and the cabin/segment if ambiguous. The new amount is validated against the airline's range; payment/confirmation completes on the operator's secu

name: get_bid_status
description: Check any bids already on a booking (submitted / accepted / declined) and the bid-eligible cabins with their ranges. Pass the sessionId from start_eligibility_check. Reflects the booking as of that secure check.

name: predict_bid_success
description: Given a SPECIFIC bid amount, returns the probability it succeeds, grounded in Plusgrade's own resolved accept/reject outcomes for this carrier and cabin — never a guess. Use this when a traveler asks something like 'will $400 get me the upgrade?' or before confirming a bid amount with place_bid. If

name: start_watch
description: Set up a Watcher Concierge: have Upgrade Agent CONTINUOUSLY watch a traveler's booking between now and departure and surface improvements to their trip — a better seat (even window-vs-middle), an empty adjacent seat, a cabin upgrade, lounge, or fast track — acting within a budget or notifying them.

name: get_watch_status
description: Check a Watcher Concierge watch (created with start_watch). Returns its status, what it's watching for, and any improvements found so far. Pass the token from start_watch.

name: ask_upgrade_agent
description: Answer a general (not booking-specific) travel-upgrade question — how bid/instant/points upgrades work, eligibility rules, bidding strategy, and which airlines, cruise lines, or rail operators offer upgrades. Use this for informational questions only. To check a SPECIFIC booking, use start_eligibili

name: partner_question_insights
description: Report what travelers and AI agents are asking about a specific partner's upgrade programs: total volume, the most frequent questions, which agents are asking, and which answers were strong vs. which need review. Pass the partner name (e.g. 'Air Canada', 'MSC Cruises').

name: usage_totals
description: Running totals across the whole system: how many questions have been asked, and how many were answered successfully (quality at or above the success threshold).

name: list_partners
description: List the travel operators that offer upgrade programs, with optional filters. Returns each operator's name, IATA/code, vertical, region, and the upgrade products it offers. Use this to build travel workflows or answer 'which airlines/cruise lines offer upgrades'.

name: get_partner_info
description: Get the full upgrade profile for one operator — products offered (with the official page URL), loyalty program, cabin paths, how to access, and summary. More detailed than ask_upgrade_agent. Accepts an IATA code or a name (e.g. 'AC' or 'Air Canada').

name: get_upgrade_pricing
description: Answer 'how much does it cost to upgrade on <airline>?' WITHOUT needing a booking. Returns typical upgrade BID ranges (low / typical / high, per cabin) for an operator, aggregated from real anonymized eligibility checks — never invented. Accepts an IATA code or a name (e.g. 'LH' or 'Lufthansa'). Use

name: search_upgrade_options
description: Find operators that offer upgrades, filtered by vertical, region, and/or product. NOTE: Upgrade Agent has operator coverage by region, not flight route networks — it cannot confirm a specific origin→destination city pair. If origin/destination are given, results are operators offering upgrades (opti

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "start_eligibility_check" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Check whether a specific booking is eligible for an upgrade. THIS IS HOW YOU CHECK A BOOKING. You do NOT need access to any reservation system, booking database…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: check_upgrade_eligibility, start_eligibility_check, get_eligibility_result, place_bid, start_watch, get_partner_info · Private data: start_eligibility_check, get_watch_status · Public sink: start_eligibility_check, start_watch · Destructive: modify_bid.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: check_upgrade_eligibility, start_eligibility_check, get_eligibility_result, place_bid, start_watch, get_partner_info · Private data: start_eligibility_check, get_watch_status · Public sink: start_eligibility_check, start_watch · Destructive: modify_bid

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
